questions.main_text:
### Printing a Boarding Pass 12 p.



The following is a conversation in an airport, between an airline representative and a customer. The recording is in four sections. In each question, pay attention to the tone of voice and decide which of the customer’s responses is the most polite. You can listen to each option as many times as you like.

content.text:
### Printing a Boarding Pass 12 p.



The following is a conversation in an airport, between an airline representative and a customer. The recording is in four sections. In each question, pay attention to the tone of voice and decide which of the customer’s responses is the most polite. You can listen to each option as many times as you like.

choices.text: niiden pelättiin asettavan naiset alttiiksi vaaroille.
niiden opetus naisille aloitettiin kouluissa varsin myöhään.
joutui ennen kilpailua läpäisemään rankan kuntotestin.
yritti naamioitua mieheksi päästäkseen mukaan kilpailuun.
yritettiin väkisin poistaa juoksureitiltä kesken kilpailun.
mutta koululiikunta voi myös vahvistaa sukupuolten välisiä eroja.
tosin erilaiset liikuntavalinnat voivat johtaa sukupuolten välisiin konflikteihin.
ja niinpä monet koulut tarjoavat yhteisiä liikuntakursseja tytöille ja pojille.

questions.main_text:
– Hey, did you guys make it to Gigi’s last night?

– Oh yeah.

– Ah, sorry.

– We were there, we were all sitting down at the patio, it was a beautiful night, eating tamale pie.

– Yeah. You were eating tamale pie? I got to hang up downtown, sorry. Was Karen there?

– Yeah, Karen and Tom, everybody was there, and you know who we saw?

– Who?

– There was... it was a celebrity.

– A celebrity was at Gigi’s?

– Yeah, Victor Borgnine was there. We saw him.

– Ah, you know, I think you’re confusing two different guys, and they’re both dead.

– Oh, I think he was in town for a movie.

– Mm.

content.text:
– Hey, did you guys make it to Gigi’s last night?

– Oh yeah.

– Ah, sorry.

– We were there, we were all sitting down at the patio, it was a beautiful night, eating tamale pie.

– Yeah. You were eating tamale pie? I got to hang up downtown, sorry. Was Karen there?

– Yeah, Karen and Tom, everybody was there, and you know who we saw?

– Who?

– There was... it was a celebrity.

– A celebrity was at Gigi’s?

– Yeah, Victor Borgnine was there. We saw him.

– Ah, you know, I think you’re confusing two different guys, and they’re both dead.

– Oh, I think he was in town for a movie.

– Mm.

I want to print my boarding pass: can you do that here?

questions.main_text:
[LINK text=The passage related to question 8.2|url=http://yle.fi/plus/abitreenit/2021/Kevät/2021-03-22\_EA\_fi/index.html#8.2]




_Brandi: You remember that stray cat that Mike and I took in last week?_

_Michelle: Of course, I saw your pictures! He’s a beautiful cat!_

_Brandi: Well, actually he is a she. And the cat’s now got four kittens living under our bed._

_Michelle: Oh my goodness! And you didn’t realise when you took her in?_




How does Michelle sound? 3 p.

questions.main_text:
Hello. My name is Seshadri Ramkumar, an environmental toxicologist at Texas Tech. In my nonwovens and

advanced materials lab, my research group is studying low-grade cotton for its potential in cleaning up oil

spills. Our research shows that the low-grade cotton that gets rejected by the garment industry has a weak

structure that collapses, packing more fibers into a given area. Crude oil not only sticks to the surface, it

absorbs into the fiber. This could be good news for West Texas cotton farmers, because about 10% of the

cotton grown here is this very type.

content.text:
Hello. My name is Seshadri Ramkumar, an environmental toxicologist at Texas Tech. In my nonwovens and

advanced materials lab, my research group is studying low-grade cotton for its potential in cleaning up oil

spills. Our research shows that the low-grade cotton that gets rejected by the garment industry has a weak

structure that collapses, packing more fibers into a given area. Crude oil not only sticks to the surface, it

absorbs into the fiber. This could be good news for West Texas cotton farmers, because about 10% of the

cotton grown here is this very type.
